title: The Rainmaker's Ledger
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A crop-risk auditor finds impossible rainfall payments and follows them into a private weather empire.
long_description:
The Rainmaker's Ledger follows Nadia Kroll, an adult crop-risk auditor, through drought counties across the high plains as a crop-risk auditor finds impossible rainfall payments and follows them into a private weather empire. The inquiry moves through offices, sealed rooms, public hearings, and physical evidence, keeping its suspense grounded in work, speech, and consequence.

As Amos Reed, Inez Vale, and June Petty help test the record, Victor Hain tries to turn delay into truth. The novel is clean adult suspense about institutions, memory, and the cost of naming what everyone was paid not to see.
